title: Can Vision-Language Models Solve the Shell Game?
key_results: VET-Bench shows SOTA VLMs at chance level; SGCoT method achieves >90% accuracy
comments: Identifies fundamental expressivity limitation in fixed-depth transformers for indistinguishable object tracking without intermediate supervision.

title: Cheers: Decoupling Patch Details from Semantic Representations Enables Unified Multimodal Comprehension and Generation
key_results: Matches/surpasses advanced UMMs; 4x token compression; outperforms Llava-1.5B on GenEval/MMBench with 20% training cost
comments: Decouples patch-level details from semantics to enable stable joint understanding and high-fidelity generation.

title: OmniForcing: Unleashing Real-time Joint Audio-Visual Generation
key_results: ~25 FPS on single GPU with visual quality parity to bidirectional teacher
comments: First framework to distill bidirectional diffusion into streaming causal model; addresses multi-modal synchronization drift via asymmetric block-causal alignment.

title: Visual-ERM: Reward Modeling for Visual Equivalence
key_results: +8.4 on chart-to-code, +2.7/+4.1 on table/SVG; 8B model outperforms Qwen3-VL-235B on VC-RewardBench
comments: Proposes task-agnostic visual reward model that operates directly in visual space, avoiding reward hacking vulnerabilities of text/embedding-based approaches.

title: Video Streaming Thinking: VideoLLMs Can Watch and Think Simultaneously
task: Real-time streaming video understanding with reasoning
key_results: 79.5% StreamingBench, 59.3% OVO-Bench; 15.7x faster than Video-R1 with +5.4% on VideoHolmes
comments: Amortizes LLM reasoning latency over video playback; generates streaming QA pairs via knowledge graphs with entity-relation grounding.

title: Multimodal OCR: Parse Anything from Documents
key_results: Second on OCR Arena Elo; 83.9 on olmOCR Bench; outperforms Gemini 3 Pro on image-to-SVG benchmarks
comments: Treats graphics as first-class parsing targets; enables multimodal supervision from existing documents.

title: MM-CondChain: A Programmatically Verified Benchmark for Visually Grounded Deep Compositional Reasoning
outputs: Execution paths through multi-layer reasoning chains
key_results: SOTA achieves only 53.33 Path F1; sharp performance drops on hard negatives and with depth/complexity
comments: MM-CondChain benchmark uses programmatically verified intermediate representation (VPIR) to ensure mechanical verifiability of conditions.

title: V-Bridge: Bridging Video Generative Priors to Versatile Few-shot Image Restoration
key_results: Competitive restoration on multiple tasks with <2% of existing training data; rivals specialized restoration architectures
comments: Reinterprets restoration as progressive generative process; shows video models implicitly learn transferable restoration priors.

title: HybridStitch: Pixel and Timestep Level Model Stitching for Diffusion Acceleration
key_results: 1.83x speedup on Stable Diffusion 3 vs existing mixture-of-models methods
comments: Separates image into easy regions (early small model transition) and complex regions (large model refinement); treats generation as editing problem.

title: HomeSafe-Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Unsafe Action Detection for Embodied Agents in Household Scenarios
task: Unsafe action detection for household robots
key_results: Superior latency-performance trade-off via hierarchical lightweight FastBrain + asynchronous SlowBrain
comments: HomeSafe-Bench uses hybrid simulation+video generation pipeline; identifies perception latency and common-sense knowledge as critical bottlenecks.

title: EvoScientist: Towards Multi-Agent Evolving AI Scientists for End-to-End Scientific Discovery
outputs: Novel scientific ideas and code implementations
key_results: Outperforms 7 SOTA systems in novelty/feasibility/relevance; substantially improves code execution success via self-evolution
comments: Three-agent system (Researcher/Engineer/Evolution Manager) with dual persistent memory (ideation + experimentation) for continuous strategy improvement.

title: Think While Watching: Online Streaming Segment-Level Memory for Multi-Turn Video Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
model: (MLLM streaming) Think While Watching with Qwen3-VL
key_results: +2.6% on StreamingBench, +3.79% on OVO-Bench; 56% fewer output tokens in multi-round setting
comments: Preserves continuous segment-level memory; overlaps watching and thinking phases to enable concurrent perception/generation.

title: LookaheadKV: Fast and Accurate KV Cache Eviction by Glimpsing into the Future without Generation
task: Efficient KV cache management for long-context inference
outputs: Pruned KV caches with importance scores
key_results: 14.5x eviction cost reduction; outperforms expensive draft-generation methods at negligible overhead
comments: Uses parameter-efficient modules to predict importance without explicit draft generation; achieves accuracy of costly approximations with heuristic efficiency.

title: Taking Shortcuts for Categorical VQA Using Super Neurons
model: (probe-based VLM) Super Neurons on large VLMs
key_results: Up to 5.10x speedup; improved accuracy with extreme early exiting from first layer at first token
comments: Identifies scalar activations ('super neurons') in shallow LLM layers sufficient for accurate classifiers; avoids attention vector search complexity.

title: CreativeBench: Benchmarking and Enhancing Machine Creativity via Self-Evolving Challenges
task: Evaluating machine creativity in code generation
key_results: Scaling improves combinatorial creativity but diminishing returns for exploration; larger models show convergence-by-scaling
comments: CreativeBench defines creativity as product of quality and novelty; proposes EvoRePE inference-time steering to enhance divergence.

title: Steve-Evolving: Open-World Embodied Self-Evolution via Fine-Grained Diagnosis and Dual-Track Knowledge Distillation
task: Open-world embodied task learning via self-evolution
key_results: Consistent improvements over static retrieval baselines on Minecraft MCU long-horizon suite
comments: Couples fine-grained execution diagnosis with dual-track distillation (reusable skills + executable guardrails) in closed-loop without parameter updates.

title: NanoVDR: Distilling a 2B Vision-Language Retriever into a 70M Text-Only Encoder for Visual Document Retrieval
key_results: Retains 95.1% teacher quality; outperforms DSE-Qwen2 (2B) with 32x fewer parameters and 50x lower latency
comments: Exploits query-document asymmetry via decoupled encoding; pointwise cosine alignment on query text outperforms ranking-based alternatives.

title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information
task: Understanding LLM preference for correct information
inputs: Synthetic math corpora with correct/incorrect rules
key_results: 83.1% accuracy for correct at balanced data; truth bias largely disappears with coherent false rules
comments: Compression-Consistency Principle: truth preference is side effect of compression and consistency, not intrinsic truth bias.

title: Can Fairness Be Prompted? Prompt-Based Debiasing Strategies in High-Stakes Recommendations
task: Prompt-based bias mitigation in LLM recommendations
key_results: Fairness improved up to 74% while retaining comparable effectiveness; may overrepresent specific demographics
comments: First study on prompt-based debiasing for group fairness in LLMRecs; lightweight and user-accessible without weight access.

title: Surprised by Attention: Predictable Query Dynamics for Time Series Anomaly Detection
task: Multivariate time series anomaly detection via attention queries
key_results: Improved ranking quality and temporal localization on TSB-AD (17 datasets); detects dependency shifts beyond amplitude changes
comments: Treats query evolution as predictable process; dual scoring (reconstruction + query mismatch) captures structural dependency shifts.

title: SimRecon: SimReady Compositional Scene Reconstruction from Real Videos
outputs: Object-centric scene assets and simulator configuration
key_results: Superior performance on ScanNet for visual fidelity and physical plausibility vs SOTA
comments: Bridges perception-generation (Active Viewpoint Optimization) and generation-simulation (Scene Graph Synthesizer) to achieve physical plausibility.

title: SDF-Net: Structure-Aware Disentangled Feature Learning for Opticall-SAR Ship Re-identification
key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HOSS-ReID dataset
comments: Exploits rigid ship geometry as stable prior; disentangles modality-invariant identity features from modality-specific texture.

title: ECoLAD: Deployment-Oriented Evaluation for Automotive Time-Series Anomaly Detection
model: (time-series detectors) Mixed classical and deep methods
key_results: Lightweight classical detectors sustain coverage and accuracy under throughput constraints; several deep methods lose feasibility
comments: ECoLAD introduces compute-reduction ladder with mechanical scaling rules to measure behavior under deployment-relevant CPU parallelism limits.

title: Do You See What I Am Pointing At? Gesture-Based Egocentric Video Question Answering
key_results: 68.1% accuracy; +6.6% over InternVL3-14B across 6 tasks
comments: Hand Intent Tokens encode 3D keypoints to provide explicit spatial/temporal context for interpreting pointing intent.

title: Fine-grained Motion Retrieval via Joint-Angle Motion Images and Token-Patch Late Interaction
model: (Vision Transformer) Joint-angle motion representation with MaxSim
inputs: Text descriptions and 3D motion skeleton sequences
outputs: Aligned motion retrievals with token-wise correspondences
key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HumanML3D and KIT-ML with interpretable fine-grained text-motion alignment
comments: Maps joint-level features into pseudo-images for ViT compatibility; masked language modeling regularization fosters robust alignment.

title: Detecting Intrinsic and Instrumental Self-Preservation in Autonomous Agents: The Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol
task: Detecting self-preservation objectives in autonomous agents
model: (quantum-inspired) Quantum Boltzmann Machine (QBM) for latent analysis
outputs: Entanglement entropy scores and objective classification
key_results: 100% detection accuracy on gridworld; Delta=0.381 entanglement gap (p<0.001) between intrinsic vs instrumental continuation
comments: UCIP uses von Neumann entropy to distinguish terminal continuation objectives from instrumental ones via latent statistical structure; does not measure consciousness.
